senses.definition: a naive, inept, or hapless person who tends to be unlucky or make blundering mistakes
examples: He tripped over his own feet again—what a shlemiel.
In the story, the clumsy waiter is a classic shlemiel.

senses.definition: plural of shlemiel; multiple naive or hapless people who tend to blunder
examples: The two shlemiels tried to fix the machine but made things worse.
In the troupe, several shlemiels shared the stage mishaps.

senses.definition: a comic or chaotic mishap; a situation that is confused or disorganized
examples: What a shlemozzle at the party—everything went wrong.
The project became a shlemozzle after the miscommunications.

senses.definition: to have caused or become involved in a shlemozzle; to have entangled in a comic mess
examples: The event was shlemozzled by miscommunication from the start.
He shlemozzled the situation further with a rushed decision.

senses.definition: plural of shlemozzle; multiple chaotic or messy situations
examples: The festival descended into several shlemozzles due to rain.
We faced a series of shlemozzles during the move.

senses.definition: the act of creating or getting caught in a shlemozzle; causing or experiencing comic chaos
examples: They were shlemozzling the event with last-minute changes.
We spent the afternoon shlemozzling around trying to fix it.

senses.definition: to carry or haul something heavy or tedious, especially with effort
examples: I had to shlep my suitcase up three flights.
We shlepped all the boxes to the attic.

senses.definition: a long, tedious journey or burden; something burdensome to carry
examples: What a shlep this drive is.
That project was a real shlep.

senses.definition: a person who shlepps; a dull or slow person; a plodder
examples: He's a bit of a shlepp when it comes to doing tasks.
Don't be such a shlepp about it.

senses.definition: plural of shlepp; people who shlep or are slow/pedestrian
examples: The movers and other shleppers carried boxes quietly.
Two shleppers struggled with the couch.

senses.definition: more slow, tedious, or burdensome to carry than another
examples: This route is shleppier than the direct one.
The afternoon felt shleppier with the heavy rain.

senses.definition: most slow, burdensome, or tedious to carry
examples: That was the shleppiest errand of the day.
We took the shleppiest route to avoid traffic.

senses.definition: carrying or hauling something heavy or tedious; the act of traveling with effort
examples: We spent the afternoon shlepping bags up the stairs.
They were shlepping from one meeting to another all day.

senses.definition: plural of shlepps; people who carry or drag heavy loads; slow or tedious workers
examples: The team of shlepps trudged along with tools.
Two shlepps joined the movers for the long haul.

senses.definition: unimpressive, dull, or lazy; sluggish
examples: That performance was shleppy and uninspired.
He wore a shleppy expression all day.

senses.definition: plural of shlep; a burden or person who is slow; a dull obligation
examples: The weekly chores are such shleps.
We hauled the equipment, two shleps in total.

senses.definition: a constantly unlucky or perpetually unfortunate person who seems to attract misfortune
examples: He's a shlimazel when it comes to technology—always breaking something new.
In Jewish humor, a shlimazel is someone who can't catch a break.

senses.definition: plural of shlimazel; multiple people who are unlucky
examples: The trio are a bunch of shlimazels when it comes to tech.
The group consisted of several shlimazels attempting to fix the machine.

senses.definition: a person who is perpetually unlucky; a shlimazel
examples: Don't be a shlimazl—read the instructions first.
He had the reputation of being a shlimazl among his friends.

senses.definition: low-quality, sensational, or tacky entertainment or goods; something considered shoddy
examples: The movie was pure shlock from start to finish.
They sold shlock at the flea market.

senses.definition: of low quality; tawdry or sensational in a cheap way
examples: a shlock movie
shlocky decorations

senses.definition: more low-quality or tacky; more sensational
examples: The sequel looks shlockier than the first film.
Her outfit was shlockier than necessary for the event.

senses.definition: most low-quality or tacky; most sensational
examples: That was the shlockiest finale I've ever seen.
They wore the shlockiest outfits at the party.

senses.definition: plural of shlock; cheap or tawdry entertainment or goods
examples: The market sold several shlocks, including cheesy posters.
Don't buy those shlocks for your room.

senses.definition: characterized by low quality or cheap sensationalism
examples: The poster was shlocky and loud.
They released a shlocky thriller.
